channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to Build a Profitable Micro-SaaS in 2026 Using AI (A Developer's Guide)
item.description: The landscape of software development and side hustles has shifted dramatically. Welcome to 2026, where you no longer need a massive team, venture capital, or months of development time to launch...

item.title: TLS Certificates for Internal Services Done Right: A Comprehensive Guide for Secure DevOps
item.description: Originally published on tamiz.pro. Securing internal service-to-service communication is paramount in modern distributed systems. While external traffic often benefits from well-established public...

item.title: Hyper - distributed Firecracker microVM orchestrator written in Elixir
item.description: Hey everyone! One of the problems I ran into is that a large part of the VM-provider ecosystem is currently paid closed-source SAAS products with varying degrees of reliability. I wanted an OSS...

item.title: How to stop Meta’s AI image generator from using your Instagram photos
item.description: Meta's new AI image generator is using your public Instagram photos unless you opt out. Here's how to do that.

item.title: Beyond the Hype: How to Actually Maximize Cursor for High-Performance Vibe Coding
item.description: 1. You Are an Architectural Supervisor Now The first thing to internalize: your job is no longer to write lines of code. Your job is to describe systems, review outputs, and reject things that...

item.title: Golden Images com Packer + Ansible: como automatizamos provisionamento e centralizamos configurações
item.description: O problema Toda vez que uma máquina nova precisa subir e o processo depende de alguém lembrar os passos certos, você já tem um problema. Foi exatamente isso que eu vivi trabalhando com EC2...

item.title: The 3 Types of AI Users Dominating the Software Engineering Landscape
item.description: I was scrolling through my usual feed recently, looking for something to distract me from a failing compilation loop, when I stumbled across an interesting perspective on how our relationship with...

item.title: Why Most AI Prompts for Developers Don't Work (and How to Fix Them)
item.description: You've seen the lists. "100 ChatGPT Prompts for Developers." "Ultimate Prompt Pack for Coders." You try one, get a generic response that doesn't understand your stack, your codebase, or your...

item.title: Google’s Nest Thermostat has hit its best price of the year
item.description: If you’re looking for a relatively affordable way to cut down on cooling costs, Google’s Nest Thermostat can help. It’s packed with smart controls and energy-saving features, and right now it’s on...

item.title: Day 1 - Basics of HTML & CSS
item.description: Today I started learning html and css. I have studied html well in my college days and now I recalled it, but I have only less knowledge in css. So today I started doing more efforts to learn css....

item.title: React Auth Emails Without State Drift
item.description: If your React auth flow sends invite, magic-link, or verification emails, the fragile part usually is not the SMTP step. It is the gap between UI state and the message that actually lands. A lot...

item.title: Nadie te dice esto sobre la IA: el verdadero cuello de botella es la electricidad (y en LATAM es peor)
item.description: Stargate consume 1.2 gigavatios. Eso es lo que usan 313,000 casas en Estados Unidos. Y es solo un proyecto de los muchos que OpenAI y Softbank están construyendo en Texas. Mientras todos discuten...

item.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
item.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

item.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
item.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the...

item.title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
item.description: Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means...

item.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
item.description: About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the...

item.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description: GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor...

item.title: The 1X Neo Robot Has Freaky Fast Fingers
item.description: The soft, oddly intimate home-chore robot has been given some very tactile hands.

item.title: Schlage’s Sense Pro unlocks the door so I don’t have to
item.description: The Schlage Sense Pro is a beautiful smart lock. Sleek, discreet, and simple to use, it's Schlage's smartest lock to date. Thanks to ultra-wideband (UWB), it unlocks as I walk up to my front door;...

item.title: ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description: Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it. This week is full of that kind...

item.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
item.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
item.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

item.title: Sonos Ace wireless headphones are steeply discounted
item.description: If you’re looking for a discount on a great pair of active noise-canceling headphones for your summer adventures (even if they’re indoors), you’re in luck. The Sonos Ace headphones in black are...

item.title: The PocketMage resurrects the PDA with an e-paper screen
item.description: Personal digital assistants like the iconic Palm Pilot were one of many devices we thought went extinct with the arrival of the smartphone. But similar to Canon resurrecting a nearly decade-old...

item.title: Sony brings back the superzoom RX10 with a stacked sensor and a high price
item.description: Sony is bringing back the RX10 superzoom camera after a nearly nine-year gap between models. The newly announced RX10 V retains the same 24-600mm equivalent f/2.4-4 Zeiss Vario-Sonnar 25x zoom...

item.title: Comcast is breaking up with NBCU. Why did it ever buy it in the first place?
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Peter Kafka, who is chief correspondent at Business Insider and host of Channels, a podcast about the media industry. And it’s a big week for the media industry...

item.title: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
item.description: After reentering the AI race with its first in-house Muse Spark model in April, Meta is now opening up the doors to developers with a new model that can plug into AI coding software with the new...

item.title: ICE agents are making house calls for online critics
item.description: A few hours after checking into a hotel in New York City, David Streever woke up to a call from the front desk saying someone was looking for him. Streever had just landed on a return trip from...

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
item.description: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.
